title: CASEF v0.6.1 common actor identity definitions
$comment: Development artifact: not represented as released until the immutable v0.6.1 Git tag exists. Actor definitions are role-bound composition bases and do not independently establish authority, eligibility, expertise, or qualification.

actor_kind.description: Exact common CASEF v0.6.1 accountable-actor kinds. An evaluated model is not automatically an accountable CASEF actor.

role_identifier.description: Role identifier, not a personal name. CASEF v0.6.1 does not create one universal actor-role enum. Exact record or protocol owners may narrow or const-bind the role; examples such as PI, RATER, EXECUTION_OPERATOR, and VALIDATOR do not authorize those roles in every object.

actor_identity_base.description: Open common actor-identity composition base. actor_id is a stable public operational identifier, and public operational content does not use a personal name; a private controlled identity mapping may exist outside the public repository. role_id binds the responsibility represented in the containing object. Actor identity alone does not prove authority, eligibility, expertise, independence, approval, or successful execution. Explicit personal-name and contact keys are structurally prohibited, but semantic privacy still requires review. Final record-specific schemas declare authorized additional fields and close the complete boundary.
actor_identity_base.$comment: This base intentionally remains open for explicitly declared owner-specific composition. Its openness is not authorization for arbitrary canonical extension fields.

human_actor_identity_base.description: Open human-actor identity composition base. Human identity does not establish qualification, independence, approval authority, or decision authority. Rater qualifications, approval basis, conflicts, eligibility, and authorization basis remain owner-specific fields in later schemas.

tool_actor_identity_base.description: Open tool-actor identity composition base. A tool may prepare, record, capture, or produce data where an owning contract permits. A tool does not become a human approving authority, PI, rater, or qualification decision authority merely through this identity structure.
